title: When do you pick LoRA over full fine-tuning?
prompt:
When do you pick LoRA over full fine-tuning?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
LoRA when: dataset is modest (hundreds to low thousands of examples), you want single-GPU efficiency, you need to serve multiple adapted versions from one base model, or you're adapting style/behavior rather than fundamentally changing capabilities. Full fine-tuning only when: very large dataset (100K+), need maximum performance shift, and have the compute budget for multi-GPU training.

**Takeaway:** Default to LoRA/QLoRA unless you have a very large dataset and compelling reason for full fine-tuning.
hint_notes: Default to LoRA/QLoRA unless you have a very large dataset and compelling reason for full fine-tuning.
